sourceMovie.title: Re-Animator
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Re-Animator (1985) as a science fiction theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The science fiction and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Night of the Creeps
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Re-Animator clicked for you, Night of the Creeps (1986) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and comedy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Bride of Re-Animator

faq.question: What should I watch after Re-Animator?
faq.answer: Try Night of the Creeps, Bride of Re-Animator, Frankenhooker—each shares DNA with Re-Animator while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Re-Animator?
faq.answer: Re-Animator (1985) is best described as Science Fiction, Comedy, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Re-Animator part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Re-Animator Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
